attributes.description: Alerts when peak read I/O latency exceeds a threshold. Uses the Maximum statistic for worst-case tail latency. Correlate with DiskQueueDepth and ReadIOPS for storage bottlenecks.
attributes.name: [AWS RDS OTel] Read latency high

investigation_guide.blob:
## Read Latency High

### Triage
1. Check `DiskQueueDepth` — climbing queue depth with plateauing `ReadIOPS` indicates I/O saturation.
2. On gp2 volumes, inspect `BurstBalance` for depleted burst credits.
3. Review provisioned IOPS vs actual `ReadIOPS`/`WriteIOPS`.
4. Sustained latency above your workload baseline can indicate storage contention.

### Mitigation
- Upgrade storage type (gp2 → gp3/io2) or increase provisioned IOPS.
- Reduce read-heavy query load or add read replicas.
- Investigate missing indexes causing excessive table scans.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.rds.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/RDS"
    AND MetricName == "ReadLatency"
    AND stat == "Maximum"
| STATS max_read_latency = MAX(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/ReadLatency`)
    BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region
// Seconds per I/O — adjust sensitivity (default: 50 ms)
| WHERE max_read_latency > 0.05
| SORT max_read_latency DESC
